title: Choosing a Hard Conversation Time
theme: Timing and planning using emotion cues

narrative.context: You need to have a difficult talk with a friend. You feel nervous and you notice they seem stressed today too.
narrative.prompt: How do you use emotional information to choose the best approach?

options.label: Use both states to plan a calmer time
options.description: Recognize mutual stress; propose a time when both can be calm and private, and prepare 2 key points.

ei.rationale: Uses emotional states to optimize timing and clarity—emotion guides effective planning.

options.label: Bring it up quickly to get it over with
options.description: Start the conversation immediately even if both feel tense.

ei.rationale: May reduce avoidance, but emotion is not used to improve conditions for good thinking and listening.

options.label: Wait indefinitely until you feel 'perfect'
options.description: Delay again and again because nerves persist.

ei.rationale: Anxiety blocks planning; emotion does not facilitate action.

options.label: Send a long message at midnight
options.description: Dump everything in text when emotions peak.

ei.rationale: Poor timing and unstructured emotion-driven communication reduce thinking quality.
